raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, my company received XXXX chargebacks from a customer who paid using XXXX American Express cards for legitimate XXXX  and XXXX XXXX. I submitted detailed evidenceincluding identity verification, cardholder authorization, and proof of services renderedthrough XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ), the processor. 

AMEX ruled in my favor in all XXXX cases ( totaling {$69000.00} ), and I received confirmation of the reversals. However, the cardholder refiled duplicate chargebacks through AMEX. On or around XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX withdrew the entire {$69000.00} from my business bank account, despite my having already prevailed in the disputes. 
I contacted AMEX directly and was told that only XXXX XXXX the merchant processorcan escalate the case to AMEX Fraud and Risk. I contacted XXXX XXXX multiple times via phone and their support channels, but they claimed they can not escalate or intervene, leaving me with no way to stop the repeated, unjustified chargebacks or recover the funds. 

This is a clear case of chargeback abuse and constitutes business harm. I am seeking CFPB assistance to compel XXXX XXXX  to escalate the issue properly to American Express and return the withdrawn funds
